findings.description: Numbered list items under item 5 (erosion and sediment control measures) are rendered with incorrect numbering. The PDF uses letters A through G, but the HTML renders them all as '1.' (number 1).
findings.evidence: PDF shows sub-items labeled A, B, C, D, E, F, G under item 5. HTML shows them all labeled as '1.' repeated.

findings.description: Repeated header/footer content appears between page-equivalent sections in the HTML. The full organizational header (NC DEQ logo, seal, department info, officials, date, certified mail info) is repeated at the top of the HTML conversion as a separate section before the letter content begins.
findings.evidence: PDF has a single letterhead on page 1. HTML has a separate title page with full header block including logos, department info, officials' names, and date repeated before the letter content.

findings.description: The numbered sub-items under item 5 (erosion and sediment control measures) are rendered as a numbered list (1, 1, 1...) instead of lettered items (A through G) as in the PDF. The HTML contains a comment '<!--OL_TYPE_A-->' but does not actually render as lettered items.
findings.evidence: PDF shows items labeled A through G under item 5. HTML shows them all numbered '1.' with a comment tag <!--OL_TYPE_A--> that has no effect on rendering.

findings.description: The page footer information (DEQ logo, department name, address, and phone number) appears both at the bottom area of the HTML and again near the top, duplicating content that only appears once as a footer in the PDF.
findings.evidence: The HTML includes a block with 'North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Division of Energy, Mineral and Land Resources | 512 North Salisbury Street | 1612 Mail Service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699 1612 | 919.707.9200' near the top of the document alongside the DEQ logo image. This is the page footer from the PDF duplicated into the body content area.

findings.description: The state official information (Governor, Secretary, Acting Director) and the state seal/logo are rendered as body content near the top. In the PDF, these are part of the letterhead header. While acceptable reformatting, combined with the footer duplication, this creates a cluttered opening.
findings.evidence: HTML has separate paragraphs for 'ROY COOPER Governor', 'MICHAEL S. REGAN Secretary', 'BRIAN WRENN Acting Director' as body content, plus the NORTH CAROLINA Environmental Quality text.
